readerView.agentInstruction: Do not read, parse, or update this HTML file during normal task work. Agents coordinate by updating board.json only.

transitionApi.apiLog: task-board/task-board-api.log records every GET and POST API request as JSON Lines with timestamp, method, endpoint, task ID, agentName, status, and error details.
transitionApi.agentInstruction: When the local task-board backend is running, prefer these endpoints for task creation, edits, status changes, and cleanup so claims and moves are atomic. If the backend is not running, update board.json directly using the same workflow rules.
transitionApi.agentName: Every agent must register at chat start with POST /api/register-agent (personalName, model, role) to receive an agentId, then include that agentId in every later API payload.
transitionApi.titleValidation: Every task must have a real, nonblank title. Untitled, Untitled task, and blank titles are invalid; the API rejects them and the viewer hides malformed records.

roleSeparation.webFrontendAuditor: Web Front-End Auditor is an upstream handoff role. It inspects the running UI and writes Markdown handoffs, but never creates tasks, claims work, moves cards, edits code, or reviews completed work.
roleSeparation.planner: Planner is planning-only. It records requirements in board.json, but never switches into Worker or Reviewer behavior in the same chat.
roleSeparation.worker: Worker execution starts only in a separate Worker chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Worker role.
roleSeparation.reviewer: Reviewer review starts only in a separate Reviewer chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Reviewer role.
roleSeparation.hardRule: Planner must not review completed work, claim tasks, edit product code, run implementation, or make code changes. Reviewer may update only board.json (plus reference images when recorded on a follow-up task). Reviewer must not plan general work, claim todo tasks, edit code, edit docs, edit the HTML viewer, run implementation, or make code changes.

review.title: Add viewer pause controls and countdown
review.summary: Add owner-facing Pause +1h and Resume now controls to the task board viewer, with visible paused status and countdown.
requirements: Add a compact global pause control to the viewer that is visible near the existing board/dispatch controls without covering lane content.
Provide a single Pause +1h button that calls the backend accumulator endpoint.
Provide a Resume now button while paused that clears the active pause.
Show paused state, pausedUntil, and a live remaining-time countdown while the board is paused.
Disable or visually mark Worker/Reviewer Spawn buttons and Auto controls as paused while the pause is active, using backend pause status as the source of truth.
Keep the design responsive at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths and consistent with the existing viewer styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Pause +1h updates the viewer to show an active pause with about one hour remaining, and clicking it again increases the displayed remaining time by about one hour.
Clicking Resume now clears the paused UI state and re-enables spawn/dispatch controls without a page reload.
While paused, Worker and Reviewer spawn controls communicate that work is paused and do not appear actionable as normal spawn buttons.
The countdown updates in place and stays consistent after auto refresh or manual board refresh.
At 1920x1080, 1440x900, 768x1024, and 390x844, pause controls do not overlap lane headers, dispatch controls, task cards, side tabs, or settings panels.
Validation includes viewer inline script syntax check and browser inspection of the pause control at desktop and mobile widths.

inspectionTargets.label: Viewer pause controls
inspectionTargets.state: Open the viewer, click Pause +1h multiple times, observe the countdown and disabled Worker/Reviewer Spawn and Auto controls, then click Resume now.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm the pause controls remain compact, accumulation adds one hour per click, Resume now clears the paused state without reload, and no toolbar/lane/sidebar overlap appears at the listed viewports.

review.notes:
Depends on backend pause/status/resume endpoints from TASK-20260624-001. Keep the UI simple: one Pause +1h button that accumulates the pause period, plus Resume now.

Claiming viewer pause controls. Existing claimed work is TASK-20260624-003; this task is viewer-focused and only overlaps server.py for pause API integration from TASK-20260624-001.

Unclaimed (2026-06-24T11:34:31-07:00) by owner via task board viewer: Worker agent was killed before finishing.

Completed viewer pause controls: Pause +1h and Resume now render in the toolbar, live countdown updates in place, and Worker/Reviewer Spawn plus Auto controls are disabled and labeled while paused. Validation: inline script syntax check passed; BrowserOS interaction verified pause, accumulation, disabled controls, and resume; Chrome screenshots inspected at 1920x1080, 1440x900, 768x1024, and 390x844. Board pause status was resumed to unpaused after validation.

done.title: Document pause and resume workflow rules
done.summary: Update workflow documentation and role docs to explain server-enforced pause behavior, hard stops, and log-assisted resume rules.
requirements: Update workflow overview and API guide to document pause status, Pause +1h accumulation, Resume now, hard-stop behavior, pausedRuns, and resume-priority dispatch.
Update Worker and Reviewer role docs to explain what a paused response from claim endpoints means and what to do when launched in resume mode.
Make clear that backend pause enforcement blocks new claims/spawns; role guidance is secondary and not the enforcement mechanism.
Document that hard stop only targets backend-spawned hidden Worker/Reviewer processes and does not kill manual terminal/chat agents.
Document that resumed agents use board state as source of truth and prior logs as context, especially for known currentTaskId cases.
Add any necessary troubleshooting notes for rate-limit pauses, expired pauses, failed resume spawns, and clearing stale pausedRuns.
acceptanceCriteria: workflow/workflow-overview.md describes the global pause lifecycle and how it interacts with auto-dispatch, manual spawn, claims, checkpoint endpoints, hard stop, and resume.
workflow/api-guide.md lists the new pause/status/resume/hard-stop endpoints and expected payload/response behavior.
roles/worker.md and roles/reviewer.md explain paused claim responses and resume-mode expectations without implying agents can bypass backend enforcement.
Docs clearly state Pause +1h accumulates one hour per click from max(now, pausedUntil).
Docs clearly state logs are context for resume but board state remains the source of truth.
Documentation changes are consistent with the implemented endpoint names and fields from the related backend/UI tasks.

done.title: Allow failed paused-run resumes to be retried
done.summary: Make failed paused-run resume attempts retryable while preserving visible failure details and successful-resume dedupe behavior.
requirements: Provide an owner-accessible retry path for pausedRuns entries whose resume spawn failed, without requiring manual JSON edits.

Ensure Resume now or the next unpaused auto-dispatch cycle can retry a failed pausedRuns entry when appropriate, while preserving lastResumeError and lastResumeFailedAt until a later successful retry clears them.
Do not repeatedly spawn duplicate resumed agents after a successful retry; successful resumed entries must still be marked resumed or otherwise removed from the pending-resume queue.
Keep failed entries visible in /api/agents and dispatch settings with enough failure detail for the owner to decide whether to retry or clear them.
Preserve existing behavior that resumed runs are attempted before unrelated fresh Worker or Reviewer dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a pausedRuns entry whose first resume spawn raises an error, the entry records failure details and a subsequent owner retry path can attempt the same paused run again.
After a retry succeeds, the pausedRuns entry is marked resumed or removed from the retry queue and is not spawned again on the next auto-dispatch tick.
The retry fix does not regress resume-before-fresh ordering, resume prompt contents, or pause suppression while paused.
Validation includes python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py and a focused helper or endpoint test showing failure retention followed by a successful retry.

inspectionTargets.label: Failed paused-run resume retry
inspectionTargets.state: Simulate a pausedRuns entry where the first resume spawn fails, then allow the next unpaused resume attempt to run after the failure cause is cleared.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm resumeState resume-failed remains visible with lastResumeError/lastResumeFailedAt, is eligible for retry, and a successful retry marks the entry resumed, clears failure details, appends one pending spawn, and does not retry again on later auto-dispatch ticks.

done.notes:
Created from failed review of TASK-20260624-004. Evidence: in-memory helper validation printed failed-run-auto-retryable: False after a failed spawn because resumeState becomes resume-failed and paused_run_is_waiting excludes that state.

Claiming failed paused-run retry task. Current claimed overlaps server.py with viewer pause controls, but this scope is backend pausedRuns retry behavior and does not target viewer layout/control implementation.

Implemented failed paused-run retry eligibility by treating resume-failed as retryable, while successful retries still mark the entry resumed and now clear lastResumeError plus lastResumeFailedAt. Validation: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; python3 -m json.tool task-board/agent-dispatch-settings.json; focused in-memory helper test simulated spawn failure retention followed by successful retry cleanup and confirmed resumed entries are no longer retryable.

Verified python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, python3 -m json.tool task-board/agent-dispatch-settings.json, and a focused in-memory helper test covering failure retention, successful retry cleanup, and no repeated retry after resume.

done.title: Resume paused runs before fresh dispatch
done.summary: Teach resume/auto-dispatch to restart interrupted spawned agents from pausedRuns before launching fresh Worker or Reviewer work.
requirements: When pause expires or Resume now is clicked, make auto-dispatch prioritize pausedRuns before starting fresh Worker or Reviewer agents.

Build a resume start prompt that includes the original role/model/name, backendBaseUrl, prior logPath, stoppedAt, currentTaskId when known, and instructions to inspect board state and worktree before continuing.
If currentTaskId is known and the task is still claimed/reviewing by the same logical agent, instruct the resumed agent to continue that task rather than claim a new one.

If currentTaskId is missing or the task state has changed, instruct the resumed agent to reconcile from the board and log, then either continue the safest matching locked task or unregister with a clear note.
After successfully spawning a resumed run, mark the pausedRuns entry as resumed or remove it from the pending-resume queue so it is not spawned repeatedly.
If resumed spawning fails, leave the pausedRuns entry visible with failure details so the owner can retry or clear it.
Ensure fresh auto-dispatch still works normally once pausedRuns are exhausted or explicitly cleared.
acceptanceCriteria: Given one pausedRuns entry and an expired/cleared pause, the backend spawns a resumed Worker/Reviewer before spawning any unrelated fresh agent for available todo/review work.
The resumed agent prompt includes the prior log path and tells the agent to use the board as source of truth, with the log as context.
A known claimed task remains locked and the resumed Worker is directed to continue that task rather than claim another todo task first.
A known reviewing task remains locked and the resumed Reviewer is directed to finish that review rather than claim another review task first.
Failed resume spawn attempts are recorded and visible; successful resume attempts do not duplicate on the next auto-dispatch tick.
Validation includes python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py and focused helper or endpoint tests for paused-run priority, prompt contents, success cleanup, and failure retention.

inspectionTargets.label: Paused-run resume dispatch
inspectionTargets.state: Create or simulate a pausedRuns entry with resumeState waiting, ensure the board is not paused, and allow auto-dispatch to run once with additional todo/review work available.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm the paused run is spawned before fresh dispatch, the new log prompt includes the prior log and board-first guidance, successful entries are marked resumed, and failed entries keep lastResumeError. Use clearPausedRuns through dispatch settings only when intentionally clearing stale entries.

done.notes:
The resume contract should be board-first and log-assisted. Logs help the new process continue, but board state remains the authority for task ownership and allowed next actions.

Claiming backend paused-run resume dispatch. Current claimed TASK-20260624-002 is viewer-focused; this task is server-side resume dispatch and does not overlap its UI acceptance criteria.

Implemented paused-run resume dispatch before fresh auto-dispatch, resume-mode spawn prompts with prior log/stoppedAt/currentTaskId/current lock state and board-first instructions, successful resume marking with pending spawn tracking, failed resume retention with error details, and clearPausedRuns support in dispatch settings updates. Validation: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; python3 -m json.tool task-board/agent-dispatch-settings.json; focused helper tests with spawn stub covering pause suppression, resume-before-fresh ordering, no duplicate resume on later ticks, prompt contents for a locked claimed task, and failure retention; live restarted backend returned /api/pause-status and /api/agents includes pausedRuns.

The helper confirmed failed-run-auto-retryable: False because resume-failed is excluded by paused_run_is_waiting, leaving the entry clearable but not retryable through the owner workflow.

The helper confirmed failed-run-auto-retryable: False because resume-failed is excluded by paused_run_is_waiting, leaving the entry clearable but not retryable through the owner workflow.

done.title: Add hard-stop capture for spawned agents
done.summary: Add a backend-controlled hard stop for hidden spawned Worker/Reviewer processes that records enough structured context to resume them later.
requirements: Add an owner/viewer endpoint to stop backend-spawned Worker and Reviewer processes while the board is paused.
Only target hidden spawned processes tracked by the task-board backend; do not claim to stop separate manual terminal/chat agents.

Before terminating a spawned process, capture a pausedRuns entry with role, model, personalName, processId, logPath, stoppedAt, stopReason, currentTaskId when available, and current board column when inferable.
Infer currentTaskId from active registered agent state first, then from claimed/reviewing task ownership when possible, and leave it blank rather than guessing when ambiguous.
Terminate processes using a safe cross-platform sequence, with graceful termination before force kill where supported, and record the observed stop status.
Keep claimed and reviewing tasks in their locked columns when hard-stopping so another agent does not accidentally pick them up as fresh work.
Expose pausedRuns in agent/status responses so the viewer can show that interrupted runs are waiting to resume.
acceptanceCriteria: When hidden spawned Worker/Reviewer processes are running, the hard-stop endpoint attempts to stop them and records one pausedRuns entry per targeted run with logPath and any known currentTaskId.
Hard-stopping does not move claimed tasks back to todo or reviewing tasks back to review automatically.
If no backend-spawned Worker/Reviewer processes are running, the endpoint returns a clear no-op response.
Manual agents that are only registered but not tracked as pending/spawned backend processes are not killed, though their active registration may still be visible.
Paused run metadata survives backend restart and remains available for the resume task.
Validation includes python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py and focused process/status tests using a harmless short-lived or sleep command where feasible.

inspectionTargets.label: Hard stop spawned agents
inspectionTargets.state: Pause the board with POST /api/pause-plus-one-hour, ensure a backend-spawned Worker or Reviewer process is tracked in pendingSpawns, then POST /api/hard-stop-spawned-agents and inspect /api/agents plus task-board/agent-dispatch-settings.json.
inspectionTargets.notes: Hard stop is intentionally blocked unless the board is paused. It targets only backend-tracked spawned processes, records pausedRuns before stopping, and does not move claimed/reviewing tasks. Resume with POST /api/resume-now after inspection if you used a real pause.

done.notes:
This is the severe rate-limit escape hatch. It should be explicit and backend-scoped: stop hidden spawned agents the board owns, capture resume context, and avoid pretending manual chats can be killed from the viewer.

Claiming hard-stop capture work after TASK-20260624-001 moved to review; no claimed task conflicts found.

Implemented paused-only hard-stop endpoints for backend-spawned Worker/Reviewer processes, persistent pausedRuns metadata, task-context inference from active agents then claimed/reviewing locks, safe terminate/force-kill handling, zombie-aware POSIX process status, and pausedRuns exposure through /api/agents. Validation: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; python3 -m json.tool task-board/agent-dispatch-settings.json; focused temporary-server process test with a harmless sleep process covering unpaused 409 guard, paused hard-stop capture, process termination, pausedRuns persistence/exposure, no-op behavior, and claimed-task lock preservation; live restarted backend returned /api/pause-status and /api/hard-stop-spawned-agents returned 409 while unpaused.

Review approved (2026-06-24T10:45:10-07:00): Approved. Checked server implementation for pause-gated hard-stop endpoints, persistent pausedRuns metadata, active-agent-first and board-lock task inference, safe terminate/force-kill handling, locked-task preservation, and /api/agents pausedRuns exposure. Live unpaused POST /api/hard-stop-spawned-agents returned HTTP 409 without touching the tracked spawned reviewer. No-write syntax parse passed. Isolated temp-state tests with harmless sleep processes confirmed hard stop records logPath/currentTaskId/currentColumn, terminates the spawned process, removes pendingSpawns, persists pausedRuns, preserves claimed/reviewing locks, returns a clear no-op when no running spawned process remains, and falls back to board-lock inference when no active agent heartbeat exists.
done.reviewNotes: Approved. Checked server implementation for pause-gated hard-stop endpoints, persistent pausedRuns metadata, active-agent-first and board-lock task inference, safe terminate/force-kill handling, locked-task preservation, and /api/agents pausedRuns exposure. Live unpaused POST /api/hard-stop-spawned-agents returned HTTP 409 without touching the tracked spawned reviewer. No-write syntax parse passed. Isolated temp-state tests with harmless sleep processes confirmed hard stop records logPath/currentTaskId/currentColumn, terminates the spawned process, removes pendingSpawns, persists pausedRuns, preserves claimed/reviewing locks, returns a clear no-op when no running spawned process remains, and falls back to board-lock inference when no active agent heartbeat exists.

done.title: Add global pause state and claim gates
done.summary: Add backend-owned pause state so rate-limit pauses are enforced by the server, not by agent memory or role-doc discipline.
requirements: Add a persistent global pause state with pausedUntil, pausedBy, pausedAt, and pauseReason fields, stored in an appropriate task-board state file or dispatch settings structure that survives backend restart.
Add owner/viewer and API endpoints to read pause status, pause for exactly one additional hour, and resume now.
When Pause +1h is called, accumulate the pause window as max(now, current pausedUntil) plus one hour.
While paused, reject /api/claim-task and /api/claim-review with a clear paused response that includes pausedUntil and remaining time.
While paused, reject manual /viewer/spawn-agent and /api/spawn-agent requests for Worker and Reviewer roles with a clear paused response.
Do not block checkpoint/cleanup endpoints needed to leave the board consistent, including move-to-review, approve-review, request-changes, unclaim-task, heartbeat-agent, and unregister-agent.
Ensure the auto-dispatch loop observes pause state and does not spawn Worker or Reviewer agents while paused.
acceptanceCriteria: Calling the pause endpoint repeatedly extends pausedUntil by one hour per call from the later of now or the existing pausedUntil.
During an active pause, /api/claim-task, /api/claim-review, /viewer/spawn-agent, and /api/spawn-agent fail without moving tasks or starting processes, and return a human-readable pause message.
During an active pause, Worker/Reviewer checkpoint endpoints still work for already-claimed or already-reviewing tasks.
Auto-dispatch does not spawn new Worker or Reviewer agents while paused, even when auto-dispatch is enabled and matching work exists.
After pausedUntil has passed or Resume now is called, normal claim and spawn behavior resumes.
Validation includes python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py plus focused endpoint or helper tests for pause accumulation, claim rejection, spawn rejection, auto-dispatch suppression, and resume.

inspectionTargets.notes: The same endpoints are available under /viewer. Paused responses include pausedUntil, remainingSeconds, remainingText, pauseReason, and a human-readable error. The live backend was restarted after implementation.

Planned from owner request to handle rate limits with a Pause All control. Backend enforcement is required so the system does not rely on Workers/Reviewers remembering role-doc instructions.

done.title: Add restart-safe agent setup doctor
done.summary: Make new-laptop setup self-diagnosing for Codex and Claude Code spawning, including clear handling when the viewer calls a health-check route that the currently running backend does not support.
requirements: Ensure the running backend exposes /viewer/agent-health-check and /api/agent-health-check, and that the Settings Test Codex/Test Claude buttons no longer produce a generic HTTP 404 after the backend is restarted.

When the health-check endpoint is missing because the viewer HTML is newer than the running backend process, show a plain message telling the owner to restart task-board/server.py instead of only reporting HTTP 404.

Make the backend auto-detect plain codex and claude command names using PATH plus common install locations such as /opt/homebrew/bin, /usr/local/bin, ~/.local/bin, and relevant npm/Windows locations before requiring manual full-path settings.
Run the health check with the same command shape as real spawning: Codex uses exec --skip-git-repo-check with a tiny OK prompt; Claude Code uses -p with the same tiny prompt.
Give spawned Codex and Codex health checks a writable SQLite state location when CODEX_SQLITE_HOME is not already set, so a read-only ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite does not immediately kill hidden agents.
Return actionable failure messages for common first-run issues: command not found, untrusted directory, not signed in/access token missing, read-only Codex state, and timeout.
Update setup documentation so a new laptop flow is: start backend, open viewer Settings, run the setup/health check, follow only the remaining human-required prompts such as sign-in or trust.
acceptanceCriteria: Posting to /viewer/agent-health-check on a restarted backend returns JSON with ok/status/tool/command/durationMs instead of 404.

If an older backend is still running and the viewer receives 404 from /viewer/agent-health-check, the UI status explicitly says the backend likely needs to be restarted to load the health-check route.

With Codex installed in /opt/homebrew/bin but that directory absent from the Python server PATH, Test Codex and Spawn still resolve and use the executable without requiring the owner to paste the full path.

With ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite read-only but auth/config otherwise usable, Codex health checks/spawns use the workflow-provided writable SQLite state folder and do not fail with the read-only database error.
When Codex is not trusted/signed in, the health check fails with a human-readable next step rather than a hidden spawned-agent log being the only clue.
Validation includes python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, viewer inline script syntax check, and at least one local negative health-check test using a deliberately missing command.

inspectionTargets.label: Settings health checks
inspectionTargets.state: With the currently older backend still running, open Settings and click Test Codex/Test Claude; then restart task-board/server.py, refresh, and click them again.
inspectionTargets.notes: Before restart the status should say to restart task-board/server.py. After restart the endpoint should return pass/fail details and next-step suggestions instead of a generic HTTP 404.

inspectionTargets.label: Health-check API
inspectionTargets.state: After restarting task-board/server.py, POST JSON such as {"tool":"codex","command":"__missing_codex_for_negative_test__"}.
inspectionTargets.notes: The response should include ok/status/tool/command/durationMs and a suggestion for missing commands. Codex checks/spawns should use task-board/codex-sqlite-state/ when CODEX_SQLITE_HOME is unset.

done.notes:
Owner reported Test Codex/Test Claude health checks returning HTTP 404. Planner reproduced with POST /viewer/agent-health-check at 2026-06-23T22:38:19-07:00; viewer log records Unknown endpoint. Likely immediate cause is a running backend process that predates the newly added health-check route, so restart handling and clearer mismatch messaging are required.

Claiming this task after confirming no claimed-task conflicts.

Completed restart-safe agent setup doctor. Backend health checks now resolve agent commands from PATH plus common install locations, set CODEX_SQLITE_HOME for Codex health checks and hidden Codex spawns when absent, and return actionable suggestions for common setup failures. The Settings panel now shows restart guidance when /viewer/agent-health-check is missing on an older running backend and displays backend next-step suggestions. Setup docs, API docs, workflow overview, and runtime ignores were updated. Validation: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; extracted viewer inline script and ran node --check; imported task-board/server.py and called agent_health_check with a deliberately missing Codex command, which returned ok=false/status=not-found with a suggestion; curl against the current older running /viewer/agent-health-check still returns 404, confirming the restart-mismatch scenario the viewer now handles.

Checked server routes for /api/agent-health-check and /viewer/agent-health-check, verified updated handler returns 200 JSON for a missing Codex command on a fresh local server, verified stale live backend shows explicit restart guidance in Settings for both Test Codex and Test Claude, confirmed command resolution finds /opt/homebrew/bin/codex with PATH empty, confirmed Codex health/spawn env uses task-board/codex-sqlite-state when CODEX_SQLITE_HOME is unset, and ran python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py plus viewer inline script node --check.

done.title: Detect POSIX PID reuse for pending agent spawns
done.summary: Extend pending-spawn liveness detection so macOS/Linux do not treat reused or unrelated running PIDs as live pending agents.
requirements: Update the non-Windows process-status path used by spawned_process_status so it uses spawnedAt, process start time, or an equivalent guard to detect PIDs that predate the recorded spawn and mark them non-live, preferably with state pid-reused.

Ensure is_live_pending_spawn, /api/agents pendingSpawns, clean_pending_spawns, and auto-dispatch pending counts exclude PID-reused, unknown, missing-pid, exited, old, or otherwise non-working pending spawns on macOS/Linux as well as Windows.
Keep truly running pending Worker/Reviewer spawns visible and counted while within DISPATCH_PENDING_SECONDS, with existing pending-chip styling and log preview behavior preserved.
Add or run focused validation covering valid running, exited, missing-pid, unknown, old/stale, and POSIX PID-reuse/mismatched-process cases.
acceptanceCriteria: On POSIX systems, a pending spawn with recent spawnedAt but a PID for a process that started before the spawn tolerance, such as PID 1 or another unrelated already-running process, is not returned from /api/agents pendingSpawns, not rendered as a dashed lane chip, and not counted by auto-dispatch.
Exited, missing-pid, unknown, and old pending spawns remain excluded from /api/agents, viewer lane chips, and auto-dispatch counts.
A valid running pending Worker/Reviewer spawn that started at the recorded spawn time remains visible/counted until it registers, exits, or ages out.
Active registered Worker/Reviewer chips and counts still use fresh heartbeat state and do not regress.
python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, viewer inline script syntax check, and a focused helper simulation/test for pending spawn liveness pass.

referenceImages.description: Owner screenshot showing the Review lane with active Ben and a stale dashed Ada chip still visible after Ada was closed/not working.

inspectionTargets.label: Agent API pending spawn liveness
inspectionTargets.state: Run the updated backend and use pendingSpawns containing valid running, exited, missing-pid, unknown, old, and POSIX PID-reuse examples.
inspectionTargets.notes: Only valid recent running Worker/Reviewer pending spawns should appear. PID-reused, unknown, exited, missing-pid, and old entries should be omitted.

inspectionTargets.label: Viewer lane pending chips
inspectionTargets.state: Reload the viewer while the updated /api/agents endpoint serves mixed pending-spawn examples.
inspectionTargets.notes: The dashed lane chips should appear only for valid running pending spawns because the viewer consumes filtered pendingSpawns from the API.

inspectionTargets.label: Auto-dispatch pending cleanup and count
inspectionTargets.state: Enable Auto with available work and stale or PID-reused pendingSpawns below the max-agent limit on the updated backend.
inspectionTargets.notes: clean_pending_spawns and pending_count should ignore stale/reused/non-working entries so replacement agents can spawn.

done.notes:
Created from failed review of TASK-20260623-004. The original implementation filters stale active agents and obvious exited/unknown/missing-pid pending entries, but POSIX PID reuse remains live because generic_process_status ignores spawnedAt.

Claiming POSIX pending-spawn liveness fix.

Implemented POSIX pending-spawn PID reuse detection in task-board/server.py. generic_process_status now reads POSIX process start time with ps -o lstart, marks processes that predate spawnedAt beyond the tolerance as pid-reused, and treats unavailable start-time checks as unknown for spawned records so stale pending entries are excluded. Validation passed: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; viewer inline script syntax check with Node; focused helper simulation for valid running, exited, missing-pid, unknown, old/stale, POSIX pid-reused, clean_pending_spawns, and /api/agents pendingSpawns enrichment; real POSIX sanity check showed PID 1 as pid-reused and a fresh sleep process as running.

Review approved (2026-06-23T22:28:26-07:00): Approved. Reviewed task-board/server.py pending-spawn liveness paths and viewer consumption of filtered pendingSpawns. Verified POSIX process start-time checks mark pre-spawn PIDs non-live, is_live_pending_spawn excludes exited, missing-pid, unknown, pid-reused, and old entries, /api/agents enrichment returns only running pending spawns, and auto-dispatch cleanup/counts use the same helper. Validation passed: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; Node inline script syntax check; focused helper simulation for live/exited/missing/unknown/old/pid-reused/filtering; live /api/agents currently returns no pendingSpawns.
done.reviewNotes: Approved. Reviewed task-board/server.py pending-spawn liveness paths and viewer consumption of filtered pendingSpawns. Verified POSIX process start-time checks mark pre-spawn PIDs non-live, is_live_pending_spawn excludes exited, missing-pid, unknown, pid-reused, and old entries, /api/agents enrichment returns only running pending spawns, and auto-dispatch cleanup/counts use the same helper. Validation passed: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; Node inline script syntax check; focused helper simulation for live/exited/missing/unknown/old/pid-reused/filtering; live /api/agents currently returns no pendingSpawns.

done.title: Hide closed agents from viewer lane chips
done.summary: The viewer can show a closed or non-working agent as an open lane chip, such as the dashed Ada reviewer chip visible next to an active Ben reviewer. Closed, exited, stale, or otherwise non-working agents should not appear as available/open in the lane header.
requirements: Audit how the viewer builds Worker and Reviewer lane agent chips from active registered agents and pending spawned agents.
Do not render closed, exited, pid-reused, unknown, stale, or otherwise non-working spawned/registered agents as open lane chips.
Ensure the Review lane does not keep showing a dashed Ada chip after the Ada agent process has closed or is no longer working.
Ensure active-agent and auto-dispatch counts ignore closed/stale agents so replacement agents are not blocked by dead presence records.
Keep currently active Worker/Reviewer chips, hover log previews for valid pending agents, Spawn buttons, and Auto dispatch controls working.
acceptanceCriteria: When /api/agents reports only Ben as an active reviewer and Ada is closed or absent, the Review lane top strip shows Ben only and no Ada chip after the normal viewer refresh/poll cycle.
If a spawned Worker or Reviewer process exits before registering or stops working, its dashed pending chip is removed from the lane header or otherwise not presented as open/available.
Closed/stale pending agents are not counted toward active Worker/Reviewer limits for Auto dispatch or max-agent controls.
A valid running pending spawn still appears with the existing pending-chip styling and log preview until it registers or exits.
No regression to active registered Worker/Reviewer chips, lane header layout, Spawn, Auto, or /api/agents response handling.

referenceImages.description: Owner screenshot showing the Review lane with active Ben and a stale dashed Ada chip still visible after Ada was closed/not working.

inspectionTargets.label: Viewer Review lane agent chips
inspectionTargets.state: Reload the viewer after the task-board backend is running the updated server.py. Use a state with one live reviewer and a stale/exited pending reviewer entry such as Ada.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm the Review lane strip shows only live active or truly running pending agents; closed, exited, pid-reused, unknown, missing-pid, or stale pending entries should not render as chips.

inspectionTargets.label: Agent API presence data
inspectionTargets.state: Call /api/agents after the backend is running the updated code and pendingSpawns includes a mix of running and exited/unknown entries.
inspectionTargets.notes: activeAgents should include only fresh active registrations; pendingSpawns should include only running recent Worker/Reviewer spawns that have not registered.

inspectionTargets.label: Auto dispatch pending cleanup
inspectionTargets.state: Enable Auto with stale/exited pendingSpawns in dispatch settings and available work below the max-agent limit.
inspectionTargets.notes: Exited, reused, unknown, missing-pid, and stale pending spawns should be cleaned/ignored so replacement agents are not blocked.

done.notes:
Created from owner screenshot showing a stale dashed Ada reviewer chip next to active Ben. The backend /api/agents check during planning showed Ben as the only active reviewer, so this should be handled as stale/pending agent display and dispatch accounting rather than real active Ada presence.

Attached owner screenshot evidence and related this task to TASK-20260623-002 because both affect the Review lane header area; scope remains distinct: this task is stale agent presence/display and dispatch accounting.

Claiming stale agent chip filtering task after checking reference image and confirming no claimed conflicts.

Completed stale agent chip filtering. Added a shared live-pending-spawn check so /api/agents only exposes running, recent Worker/Reviewer pending spawns; activeAgents are also filtered by heartbeat freshness, and auto-dispatch pending cleanup/counts use the same live check. Added a defensive viewer-side pending-spawn filter so exited, pid-reused, unknown, or missing-pid pending data cannot render as lane chips. Validation: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py; node --check on extracted viewer inline script; git diff --check for task-board/server.py and task-board/viewer.html; direct helper simulation confirmed only running pending spawns pass; temporary patched /api/agents server returned active [('worker','echo','active')] and pending [].

Changes requested (2026-06-23T22:20:33-07:00): Reviewed server.py and viewer.html paths for stale active-agent filtering, pending-spawn filtering, /api/agents, and auto-dispatch counts. Passing checks: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, extracted viewer inline script node --check, git diff --check, helper simulation for injected running/exited/pid-reused/unknown/missing-pid states, and live /api/agents showing stale registered planners excluded from activeAgents. Failing evidence: on POSIX, spawned_process_status({'role':'review','spawnedAt':now,'processId':'1'}) returns {'isRunning': true, 'state': 'running', 'warning': 'permission-limited'} and is_live_pending_spawn returns true, even though PID 1 is not the spawned reviewer process and predates the spawn. In-app browser verification was unavailable because the Node REPL browser tool failed before page access with sandboxCwd metadata validation, so this decision is based on live API output and non-mutating helper/runtime checks.

Closed as replaced (2026-06-23T22:20:33-07:00): follow-up task TASK-20260623-004 replaces this failed review task.
done.reviewNotes: Reviewed server.py and viewer.html paths for stale active-agent filtering, pending-spawn filtering, /api/agents, and auto-dispatch counts. Passing checks: python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, extracted viewer inline script node --check, git diff --check, helper simulation for injected running/exited/pid-reused/unknown/missing-pid states, and live /api/agents showing stale registered planners excluded from activeAgents. Failing evidence: on POSIX, spawned_process_status({'role':'review','spawnedAt':now,'processId':'1'}) returns {'isRunning': true, 'state': 'running', 'warning': 'permission-limited'} and is_live_pending_spawn returns true, even though PID 1 is not the spawned reviewer process and predates the spawn. In-app browser verification was unavailable because the Node REPL browser tool failed before page access with sandboxCwd metadata validation, so this decision is based on live API output and non-mutating helper/runtime checks.
done.failureExpected: Pending spawned agents whose PID was reused or belongs to an unrelated/non-working process are excluded from /api/agents, viewer lane chips, and auto-dispatch counts on macOS/Linux/Windows; only the actual running spawned Worker/Reviewer process remains live.
done.failureActual: The POSIX generic_process_status path ignores spawnedAt and treats any existing PID as running. A review spawn record with a fresh spawnedAt and processId 1 returns state running and is_live_pending_spawn true, so a stale or PID-reused pending agent can still render/count as live on macOS/Linux.
done.failureDecision: Failed review because the explicit pid-reused/otherwise non-working pending-agent criterion is only covered when process_status reports pid-reused; the generic POSIX path cannot currently detect that case.

failureBrief.expected: Pending spawned agents whose PID was reused or belongs to an unrelated/non-working process are excluded from /api/agents, viewer lane chips, and auto-dispatch counts on macOS/Linux/Windows; only the actual running spawned Worker/Reviewer process remains live.
failureBrief.actual: The POSIX generic_process_status path ignores spawnedAt and treats any existing PID as running. A review spawn record with a fresh spawnedAt and processId 1 returns state running and is_live_pending_spawn true, so a stale or PID-reused pending agent can still render/count as live on macOS/Linux.
failureBrief.decision: Failed review because the explicit pid-reused/otherwise non-working pending-agent criterion is only covered when process_status reports pid-reused; the generic POSIX path cannot currently detect that case.

archived.title: Fix To Do and Review lane header control layout
archived.summary: The To Do and Review lane headers have layout problems: dispatch controls wrap into uneven rows, controls are not aligned with the lane title, count/collapse controls drift away from the header, and the Review header reserves excessive empty vertical space.
requirements: Redesign the To Do and Review lane header/control area so title, active-agent count, model selector, max-agent input, Spawn button, Auto toggle, task count, and collapse button align predictably.
Ensure controls wrap gracefully at narrower lane widths without overlapping, clipping, or creating large empty header gaps.
Keep all controls readable, clickable, and visually associated with their lane.
Apply consistent spacing and alignment between the To Do and Review headers while preserving their role-specific colors and labels.
acceptanceCriteria: At desktop viewport around 1920x1080, the To Do header controls fit cleanly without covering the lane title or drifting into the task body area.
At desktop viewport around 1920x1080, the Review header controls align cleanly and do not create a large empty header block before the review task body.
At 1440x900 and tablet widths, To Do and Review controls wrap into deliberate rows with consistent gaps and no overlap, clipping, or off-card overflow.
The task count pills and collapse buttons remain aligned with the lane header area and do not float mid-panel.
Spawn, Auto, model selector, and max-agent controls still function after the layout change.

Reserve an appropriate left gutter, reposition the tabs, or otherwise adjust the layout so the To Do lane remains fully readable and clickable.
Keep the side tabs accessible and usable after the layout change.

Completed viewer side-tab overlap fix. Added a reserved side-tab gutter for the main shell at desktop/tablet sizes and kept a smaller reserved gutter in the mobile media query. Validation: headless Chrome geometry checks at 1920x1080, 1440x900, 768x1024, and 390x844 showed Workflow/Archived tabs do not overlap the first lane and no horizontal overflow. Workflow panel and Archived tray both opened successfully after clicks at each viewport.

Changes requested (2026-06-23T21:57:50-07:00): Reviewed http://127.0.0.1:4177/viewer.html at 1920x1080, 1440x900, 768x1024, and 390x844. Geometry checks confirmed Workflow and Archived tabs do not overlap the To Do lane/header/body at all target sizes, and both Workflow and Archived panels open by pointer click. However, a stricter overflow check using Math.max(document.documentElement.scrollWidth, document.body.scrollWidth) - document.documentElement.clientWidth reports 5px overflow at 390x844. The saved mobile screenshot shows the horizontal scrollbar. The issue appears tied to mobile shell/gutter sizing using 100vw with a vertical scrollbar present.

archived.title: Fix mobile horizontal overflow from side-tab gutter

requirements: Remove page-level horizontal overflow at 390x844 caused by the mobile side-tab gutter and shell/board width calculation.
Account for the document client width/vertical scrollbar when sizing the shell; do not let the shell, board, or To Do lane extend past the document client area.
Keep Workflow and Archived side tabs visible and outside the To Do lane/header/controls/content at 1920x1080, 1440x900, 768x1024, and 390x844.
